body.title: Doc reorg — 9-pillar structure (premise, glossary, architecture, capability map, workflow, rules, tools, navigation, lessons, decisions)
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Sonnet drowned repeatedly last week — too much information in too many places (code + .md + .txt + DB + slate + decisions log + skills) with no clear load-order. Akien proposed a 9-pillar reorganization on 2026-04-27 (this conversation) that would:

  00_premise.md           — what is this and why (Igor as GPU-free mind-mimic agent)
  01_glossary.md          — vocabulary (EXISTS at lab/docs/glossary.md — keep)
  02_architecture.md      — high-level + load-bearing subsystems (lives in palace already; assemble human-readable echo)
  03_capability_map.md    — current vs planned (EXISTS at lab/docs/capability_map.md as of 2026-04-27 — keep)
  04_workflow.md          — how Akien + Claude collaborate (folds in skills as 'automated workflow steps')
  05_programming_rules.md — coding/commit/db rules (lives in palace 'theigors/rules/*'; assemble human-readable echo)
  06_tools.md             — what Claude can call (Igor + datacenter)
  07_navigation.md        — where to find code, db, runtime
  08_lessons.md           — anti-patterns + why (NEW — partially overlaps T-case-study-lessons-learned)
  09_active_decisions.md  — currently-in-force D### (small set; archive the rest)
  archive/                — old session history, dated, NOT auto-loaded

Reading budget on session start: ~3000 tokens (down from current ~8000). Anything past that loads on demand via memory_get(...).

The reorg also includes load-order hierarchy — currently /context-load reads palace + rules + slate + decisions + channel + inbox in a flat list; new shape loads 00–04 always, 05–09 lazily.

References:
- T-context-load-review (M, pending) — narrower-scope precursor focused on context-load directory tree + drift prevention.
- T-docs-live-code-rollout (L, pending) — adjacent: load-bearing subsystems get top-of-file docstrings as canonical doc.
- T-case-study-lessons-learned (L, pending) — covers the 'lessons' pillar.

Affected files:
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/00_premise.md (new)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/02_architecture.md (new — palace echo)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/04_workflow.md (new)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/05_programming_rules.md (new — palace echo)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/06_tools.md (new)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/07_navigation.md (new)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/09_active_decisions.md (new)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/archive/ (move stale slates + old session records here)
- ~/.claude/skills/context-load/SKILL.md (rewrite load order)
- ~/TheIgors/CLAUDE.md (slim further — point at 00_premise.md as the real entry)
- ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/glossary.md, capability_map.md (keep — already in shape)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: rules + subsystem docs are palace-canonical; .md files are echoes assembled by a script (consider lab/claudecode/docs_assemble.py).
- postgres-only: palace stays the source of truth.
- oop-first: not applicable — doc work.
- no-new-memory-schemas: ✓ — palace already has the data.
- test-plan-or-why-not: needs a test that the loaded reading-budget on /context-load is <= 3000 tokens.

Scope boundary:
IN: write the 7 new pillar docs, move stale history to archive/, rewrite /context-load to the new load order, slim CLAUDE.md.
OUT: rewriting palace rules content (palace-side work — separate ticket if needed), changing skills other than /context-load, retiring T-context-load-review (this ticket SUPERSEDES that scope; close it on completion).

Test plan:
- Token budget: simulate /context-load with new order; verify total token count <= 3000.
- Drift: stale-doc Step 18.6 (added in this batch) catches drift on capability_map; equivalent step needed for the others.
- Smoke: spawn a fresh Sonnet session with new docs, ask 'is X built?' for 5 random subsystems; correctness >= 4/5.

Why this is L: 7 new docs + 2 deletions + skill rewrite + smoke harness. Should be split into ~5 child tickets at sprint time, but file as L umbrella for now.

References this conversation: 2026-04-27 design discussion (Akien + CC). The 9-pillar list and rationale came from that discussion.
body.approved_plan: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
